title: DataGrid
intro: `wf-datagrid` is the workhorse for dense tabular data — the wireframe stand-in for production's GraviGrid. It's a flexbox table, not an HTML `<table>`: a header row, a body of rows, and flex-weighted cells that carry the alignment, sort, selection, and density affordances analysts scan dozens of times a day.

blocks.body:
A DataGrid is three nested pieces: `wf-datagrid` (the bordered, rounded container), a single `wf-datagrid-header`, and a `wf-datagrid-body` holding one `wf-datagrid-row` per record. Every column is a `wf-datagrid-cell` in both the header and each row — there is no `<th>`/`<td>`, so the header and row cells must use the **same `flex` weights in the same order** or the columns won't line up.

Column width comes from inline `flex` on each cell (`flex: 2` for wide text columns, `flex: 1` for narrow ones, `flex: 0 0 60px` for fixed widths). Cells truncate with an ellipsis by default (`overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap`), so a grid stays one row tall per record no matter how long the content.

Reach for `wf-datagrid` when you have **three or more rows** of structured, columnar data. Fewer than that is a `wf-card` with a list inside (§7.4), and a contract record's worth of label/value pairs is a `wf-description-list`, not a grid.

blocks.title: Sortable headers
blocks.caption: wf-datagrid-cell-sortable headers carry a wf-datagrid-sort-icon. The active column adds wf-sorted, which tints its icon primary blue; idle columns show both arrows in tertiary gray. Numerics stay right-aligned, statuses render as wf-badge pills.

blocks.title: Structural classes
blocks.intro: The grid is composed entirely from these classes plus inline `flex` on cells. Match cell order and `flex` weights between the header and every row.

rows.name: wf-datagrid
rows.when: The outer container. Carries the 1px border, 8px radius, white surface, and clips overflow so nested rows stay inside the rounded corners.

rows.name: wf-datagrid-header
rows.when: The single header row. Neutral-50 background, bottom border, 13px / 600 secondary-text labels.

rows.name: wf-datagrid-body
rows.when: Wraps the data rows in a vertical flex column.

rows.name: wf-datagrid-row
rows.when: One per record. Has a hover background and a bottom border that drops off the last row automatically.

rows.name: wf-datagrid-cell
rows.when: The base cell. Default `flex: 1`; set inline `flex` to weight columns. Left-aligned, single-line, ellipsis-truncated.

blocks.title: Cell modifiers
blocks.intro: Stack these on a `wf-datagrid-cell` to change alignment, fixed width, or affordance. Apply the same modifier to the header cell and its column's body cells so the column reads consistently.

rows.name: wf-datagrid-cell-number
rows.when: Numeric, currency, or volume columns. Right-aligns the cell (`justify-content: flex-end`) and turns on `tabular-nums` so digits align in a column.

rows.name: wf-datagrid-cell-checkbox
rows.when: The leading selection column. Fixed `flex: 0 0 48px`, center-justified, holds a `wf-checkbox-input`.

rows.name: wf-datagrid-cell-sortable
rows.when: A clickable header cell. Adds pointer cursor, disables text selection, and reserves a gap for the sort icon. Pair with a `wf-datagrid-sort-icon` span.

rows.name: wf-sorted
rows.when: Added alongside wf-datagrid-cell-sortable on the column currently driving the sort. Tints its sort icon primary blue.

rows.name: wf-datagrid-cell-actions
rows.when: A row-action cell. `flex: 0 0 auto` with a 4px gap so inline ghost buttons sit snug at the row's end.

rows.name: wf-mono
rows.when: A utility for codes, IDs, and currency — switches the cell to a monospace face with tabular figures so they align character-for-character.

blocks.title: Row selection
blocks.caption: A leading wf-datagrid-cell-checkbox column drives bulk selection. Checked rows take wf-datagrid-row-selected, which paints a light-blue (#eff6ff) background that also persists on hover.

blocks.title: Compact density
blocks.caption: wf-datagrid-compact on the container drops cell padding from 12px/16px to 8px/12px and steps header and row text down a size — the high-volume default for analyst screens.

blocks.title: Cell types & alignment
blocks.caption: Mixed cell content: an avatar in a fixed-width column, a two-line name/email stack, a wf-badge-with-dot status, a right-aligned wf-mono currency figure, and a trailing wf-datagrid-cell-actions group.

blocks.title: DataGrid tokens
blocks.intro: Surfaces and borders read from these data-display tokens, which themselves alias the neutral and primary scales. Reach for the alias, never the raw hex.

rows.use: Header row background — a faint gray that separates labels from data.
Row hover background; signals which row the pointer is on.
Selected-row background (wf-datagrid-row-selected) — a light primary tint, held even on hover.
Container border plus every header/row divider.

blocks.title: Canonical DataGrid markup
blocks.code:
Contract ID ▲
Customer
Status
Volume
CNT-2024-001
Acme Corporation
Active
2,400,000
blocks.note: Header and row cells share identical `flex` weights in identical order — that's what keeps the columns aligned, since these are flex divs, not a real table.

blocks.title: Density and selection modifiers
blocks.code:
…
…
CNT-2024-001
…
blocks.note: wf-datagrid-compact and wf-datagrid-striped are container-level modifiers; wf-datagrid-row-selected is per-row.

items.rule: Three or more rows of columnar data before you reach for wf-datagrid.
items.why: Fewer than three rows is a wf-card with a list inside, not a grid — the grid chrome costs more than it earns at that scale (§7.4).

items.rule: A single record's label/value pairs belong in a wf-description-list, not a one-row grid.
items.why: A grid is for comparing many records down columns; a lone record reads better as terms and details.

items.rule: Never render zero-data as a blank grid — swap the body for wf-datagrid-empty with a CTA.
items.why: An empty grid is a dead end; the empty state tells the user what to do next (§7.4, §4.6).

items.rule: Don't push more than one number-and-label into a wf-metric-card to dodge a grid.
items.why: MetricCard is one number, one label, one optional trend; multi-value comparison is the grid's job (§4.1, §7.4).

blocks.title: Density, alignment, and color
blocks.intro: Cross-cutting DESIGN.md rules that govern how the grid is filled in.

items.rule: Default to wf-datagrid-compact for analyst-facing tables.
items.why: Density is a feature — a senior analyst would rather see one more row than one more breath of margin (§2.5, §5.1).

items.rule: Right-align every numeric column with wf-datagrid-cell-number, and apply it to the header cell too.
items.why: Right alignment plus tabular-nums lines up magnitudes so the eye can compare figures vertically (§2.2 grid-first).

items.rule: Status cells carry a wf-badge or wf-badge-with-dot, never bare colored text.
items.why: Color is never the only signal — pair it with a label or shape so the status survives for color-blind and AT users (§3.3, §6.3).

items.rule: Status colors only mark real status; a green badge is not a decorative accent.
items.why: Status colors are reserved for status, no exceptions — dilute them and users stop reading them (§3.1, §7.2).

items.rule: Row actions are inline ghost buttons in a wf-datagrid-cell-actions cell; bulk verbs live in an ActionBar above the grid.
items.why: Single-record actions are scoped inline; collection actions belong on a wf-action-bar, not crowded into rows (§4.3).

items.do: Give the header cell and its column's body cells the same flex weight.
items.why: These are flexbox divs, not a `<table>` — mismatched weights drift the header out of line with its column.
Without wf-datagrid-cell-number the figure left-aligns and loses tabular-nums, so magnitudes no longer line up.
Color alone fails §3.3 — the badge gives the status a shape and label, and warning is a reserved status token.

items.do: wf-datagrid-empty with a CTA when there are no rows
items.why: A blank grid is a dead end; the empty state names the next action (§7.4, §4.6).

items.do: wf-datagrid for 3+ rows of comparable records
items.dont: wf-datagrid for a single record's fields
items.why: Two rows of data is a card-with-list; a lone record is a description list. The grid earns its chrome only across many rows (§7.4).

items.title: It's flexbox, not a table
items.detail: There is no `<table>`, `<th>`, or `<td>`. Columns line up only because header and row cells share inline `flex` weights in the same order. Add or reorder a column in one place and you must mirror it everywhere.

items.title: Cells truncate by default
items.detail: Every wf-datagrid-cell sets overflow:hidden, text-overflow:ellipsis, and white-space:nowrap. Long content clips to one line — to show two lines (name over email) nest a div inside the cell rather than relying on wrap.

items.title: Selected rows stay selected-blue on hover
items.detail: wf-datagrid-row-selected:hover keeps the #eff6ff selected tint instead of swapping to the neutral hover background, so a checked row reads as selected even under the pointer.

items.title: The checkbox column is a fixed 48px
items.detail: wf-datagrid-cell-checkbox is flex: 0 0 48px and center-justified — it doesn't grow with the table, so leave it out of your flex-weight budget for the data columns.

items.title: wf-sorted only tints the icon
items.detail: Adding wf-sorted recolors the wf-datagrid-sort-icon to primary blue but doesn't change the arrow glyph — you swap the glyph yourself (single ▲ / ▼ for the active direction vs. the ▲▼ idle pair).
